mechanism_narrative: A sustained multi-month decline in the effective federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and signals policy accommodation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month regardless of the broader macro regime.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.24 near recent multi-month levels
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20-day SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window signals emerging labor market deterioration that leads equity markets to price in weaker future earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: In triggered instances, forward 20-day SPX returns are positive more than 55% of the time, or the mean triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional mean return (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint deceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects a synchronized contraction in consumer and residential investment demand that is not captured by either series alone; this combined weakening predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as earnings expectations for cyclical sectors are revised down.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST show negative 3-month growth simultaneously
failure_condition: Joint negative RSAFS/HOUST growth episodes fail to produce mean 60-day forward returns below the unconditional mean at p > 0.05, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power over either series alone (delta Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production predicts positive short-horizon S&P 500 returns during late-cycle expansions with restrictive monetary policy (FEDFUNDS above 5%), because incremental supply-side strength signals resilience against tightening-induced slowdown fears; the same signal is muted or reversed in easy-money regimes where liquidity dominates price action.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with restrictive monetary policy (FEDFUNDS > 5%)
failure_condition: The sign of the INDPRO-forward return relationship does not differ significantly between restrictive (FEDFUNDS>5%) and accommodative regimes, or Rank IC in the restrictive regime subsample is below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains above 5% for a sustained period, restrictive monetary policy compresses valuation multiples and raises the probability of a credit-tightening-induced slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for at least 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns during FEDFUNDS>5% episodes are not significantly negative relative to the full-sample mean (p > 0.05), or such episodes show positive mean returns in more than 50% of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payroll employment signals expanding labor income and consumption capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week as investors extrapolate near-term earnings strength.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135354 thousand as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing PAYEMS growth and forward 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive PAYEMS growth periods do not show significantly higher forward returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales growth, approximated jointly by nominal retail sales (RSAFS) rising faster than the CPI deflator (CPIAUCSL), indicates genuine strengthening of consumer purchasing power rather than inflation-driven nominal gains, and predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month; the effect requires the joint condition since nominal RSAFS growth alone conflates price and volume effects.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth exceeds CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation
failure_condition: The real-retail-sales interaction signal produces forward 20-day returns no better than nominal RSAFS growth alone (delta Rank IC < 0.01), or the joint condition is triggered but shows negative mean returns in more than 50% of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in industrial production exceeding a meaningful threshold signals an emerging manufacturing recession that leads earnings estimates for cyclical and industrial sectors to be revised downward, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Triggered INDPRO YoY declines below -1.0% are followed by positive mean 60-day forward SPX returns in more than 50% of historical rolling 2-year windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across walk-forward evaluation.
